data.name: Use Microsoft dev-tunnels to create public URLs for remote dashboard access

have gsd autopilot process create a claude code remote session and add the url to the dashboard so users can ask questions to claude code. The claude code session will stay open so users can ask questions or restart autopilot if nessessary.

data.message: Phase 12 found, no existing CONTEXT.md. Let me check if the phase directory exists.
Good — directory exists but is empty. Now let me analyze the phase.

questions.question: Which areas do you want to discuss for Phase 12 (Claude Code Remote Session)?

options.label: Session purpose & scope
options.description: What can users do in the remote session? Just ask questions? Give instructions? Restart autopilot? What's the boundary between the session and the dashboard?

options.label: Session lifecycle
options.description: When does the session start/stop? Always running or on-demand? What happens if the session dies? Auto-reconnect?

options.label: Dashboard integration
options.description: Where does the session URL appear? Embedded iframe vs external link? How prominent? Alongside or replacing existing UI?

options.label: Session context
options.description: Does the session know about the current project/phase? Pre-loaded with context? Or a blank Claude Code session?

answers.Which areas do you want to discuss for P: Session purpose & scope, Session lifecycle, Session context, Dashboard integration

data.message: [Answer] Which areas do you want to discuss for Phase 12 (Claude Code Remote Session)? -> Session purpose & scope, Session lifecycle, Session context, Dashboard integration
Great — all four areas selected. Let's work through each one.

questions.question: What's the primary reason a user would open the remote session?

options.label: Ask questions about the build
options.description: "Why did you do X?" "What's the status of Y?" — conversational Q&A about what autopilot is doing

options.label: Give instructions to Claude
options.description: "Fix this bug", "Change the approach" — actively directing Claude Code to do work

options.label: Both equally
options.description: Session serves as both a Q&A window and a way to give Claude hands-on instructions

data.message: [Answer] What's the primary reason a user would open the remote session? -> Both equally

questions.question: Should the remote session be able to restart or control the autopilot process?

options.label: Yes, restart autopilot
options.description: User can say "restart autopilot" or "stop autopilot" in the session and it actually controls the process

options.label: No, session is independent
options.description: The session is a separate Claude Code instance — autopilot control stays in the dashboard/CLI only

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude's discretion on whether to wire up process control

data.message: [Answer] Should the remote session be able to restart or control the autopilot process? -> don't worry about claude code capability that is not your concern you should only be worried about creating the remote session and providing the URL through the dashboard.

answers.Should the remote session be able to res: don't worry about claude code capability that is not your concern you should only be worried about creating the remote session and providing the URL through the dashboard.

data.message: Understood — Phase 12 is purely about **creating the remote session and surfacing its URL**. What users do inside the session is Claude Code's concern, not ours.

questions.question: Should the remote session be created using `claude code --remote` CLI command, or the Claude Agent SDK programmatically?

options.label: CLI command (claude --remote)
options.description: Spawn `claude --remote` as a child process, capture the URL from stdout

options.label: You decide
options.description: Claude's discretion on the best approach to create the remote session

data.message:

data.message: Got it — `claude remote-control` is the command. It stays running, outputs a session URL, and users connect via browser/phone. Let me continue the discussion.

questions.question: So the approach is: autopilot spawns `claude remote-control` as a child process, captures the session URL from stdout, and stores it in state. Is there anything else you envision beyond that for session creation?

options.label: That's it
options.description: Spawn the process, capture URL, store it, show it in dashboard. Done.

data.name: have gsd autopilot process create a claude code remote session and add the url to the dashboard so users can ask questions to claude code. The claude code session will stay open so users can ask questions or restart autopilot if nessessary.
